summary: A high-performance implementation of a small-scale LLM focusing on cutting-edge training and inference optimizations. It demonstrates patterns for FP8 compute, orthogonalized optimization (Muon), and efficient KV caching tailored for Flash Attention 3.

concepts.name: GPT Model Architecture
concepts.subtitle: The core transformer implementation with sliding window attention
concepts.description: Implements a decoder-only transformer. The key insight is the use of a 'window_pattern' to mix full-context and sliding-window attention across layers, reducing memory overhead while maintaining long-range dependencies.
concepts.ask: How does the sliding window attention pattern differ across layers in this model?

concepts.name: Inference Engine
concepts.subtitle: Stateful generation manager for token production
concepts.description: Wraps the model to handle the autoregressive loop. It manages 'RowState' to track token sequences and Python block boundaries, allowing the model to switch between text generation and code execution.
concepts.ask: How does the engine track whether the model is currently generating a Python expression?

concepts.name: FA3 KV Cache
concepts.subtitle: Optimized memory layout for Flash Attention 3
concepts.description: A specialized cache that stores tensors in (B, T, H, D) format rather than the traditional (B, H, T, D). This is specifically designed for Flash Attention 3's in-place updates, maximizing GPU throughput.
concepts.ask: Why is the tensor shape in KVCache different from standard PyTorch transformer caches?

concepts.name: Task Framework
concepts.subtitle: Unified interface for SFT training and evaluation
concepts.description: Provides a base 'Task' class that allows for lightweight slicing and mixing of datasets. 'TaskMixture' and 'TaskSequence' enable complex training curricula by combining different data sources (e.g., MMLU, GSM8K).
concepts.ask: How can a developer oversample a specific dataset using the TaskMixture class?

concepts.name: Muon Optimizer
concepts.subtitle: Orthogonalized momentum for 2D parameter updates
concepts.description: A hybrid optimizer that uses AdamW for vectors and Muon for 2D matrices. Muon uses Newton-Schulz iteration to ensure updates are orthogonal, which can significantly accelerate convergence in deep networks.
concepts.ask: What is the purpose of the Newton-Schulz iteration in the Muon optimizer?

concepts.name: FP8 Quantized Linear
concepts.subtitle: Low-precision compute with high-precision master weights
concepts.description: Replaces standard Linear layers to perform matmuls in FP8 (e4m3fn) while keeping weights in FP32. It uses a custom autograd function to handle the quantization and scaling during the forward pass.
concepts.ask: How does Float8Linear maintain precision while performing computations in FP8?

concepts.name: Distributed Worker Pool
concepts.subtitle: Multi-GPU inference load balancer
concepts.description: Manages a pool of 'Worker' instances, each pinning a model replica to a specific GPU. It uses an asyncio Queue to distribute chat requests across available GPUs for parallel serving.
concepts.ask: How does the WorkerPool handle request distribution across multiple GPUs?
